shared_mechanism: mass-action multiplication with a critical threshold at unit reproduction/multiplication factor: supercritical exponential growth versus subcritical decay, plus a self-limiting final yield
brief_reason: A neutron chain reaction in a fissile assembly is governed by the multiplication factor k, the exact analogue of the reproduction number: k>1 gives an exponentially growing supercritical chain, k<1 lets the reaction die out, and k=1 is the critical boundary, mirroring the SIR threshold and its saturating final outbreak size.
